description: User-controlled {{NAME}} placeholder from $ARGUMENTS is substituted directly into a shell command template with no escaping guidance, allowing quote-breakout injection
suggested_fix: Instruct the interpreting agent to pass the argument via a shell variable with proper quoting/escaping rather than literal template substitution

description: {{PATH}} placeholder is substituted unsanitized into a curl URL/shell command with no path validation, enabling path traversal or shell injection
suggested_fix: Validate {{PATH}} matches an allowlisted pattern (e.g. categories/[0-9]{2}-[a-z-]+/[a-z-]+\.md) before interpolating into the shell command

evidence: curl -s "$GITHUB_API_BASE/categories" with response parsed via grep/sed, no auth, no pinned ref
description: Unauthenticated network call to GitHub API to list categories in remote mode
suggested_fix: Pin GITHUB_API_BASE to a tagged release/commit SHA instead of tracking the mutable main branch

evidence: curl -s "$GITHUB_API_BASE/categories/$category" with response parsed via grep/sed
description: Unauthenticated network call to GitHub API to list agent files for a category
suggested_fix: Pin GITHUB_API_BASE to a tagged release/commit SHA instead of tracking the mutable main branch

evidence: curl -sS "$url" -o "$dest_path" writes remote content directly to the install directory with no integrity check
description: Downloads a remote .md agent definition and writes it into ~/.claude/agents/ or .claude/agents/ with no checksum/integrity verification and no pinning to a commit SHA
suggested_fix: Validate downloaded content has YAML frontmatter before writing, and pin GITHUB_RAW_BASE to a tagged release/commit SHA

evidence: curl -sf --connect-timeout 5 --max-time 30 "$SUBAGENT_CATALOG_REPO_URL/README.md" -o "$SUBAGENT_CATALOG_CACHE_FILE.tmp" fetches from the mutable main ref with no integrity check
description: Unauthenticated fetch of README.md from raw.githubusercontent.com on the mutable main ref, cached locally with no signature/checksum verification
suggested_fix: Pin SUBAGENT_CATALOG_REPO_URL to a tagged release/commit SHA, or validate fetched content format before caching

evidence: No section of CLAUDE.md describes a build or run command for the project
description: CLAUDE.md has no instructions for how to build or run the project
suggested_fix: Add a short section noting there is no build step (pure Markdown) and how to install/try an agent (e.g. via install-agents.sh)

evidence: No section of CLAUDE.md describes how to run tests
description: CLAUDE.md has no test command instructions
suggested_fix: Add a section describing how to validate a new/edited subagent file, or state explicitly that there is no automated test suite

evidence: No prerequisites/setup section present in CLAUDE.md
description: CLAUDE.md has no section covering required tools, versions, or setup steps
suggested_fix: Add a short Prerequisites section (e.g. bash, curl for install-agents.sh remote mode)

evidence: Line 58: 'Format for main README: `- [**agent-name**](path/to/agent.md) - Brief description`' preceded by 'Add link in appropriate category (alphabetical order)' with no measurable criteria for 'appropriate'
description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' used without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate category' with 'the category matching the agent's role, per the 10 categories listed above'

description: tools/subagent-catalog/*.md commands are not referenced by any plugin.json but are documented as a separately-installed tool in their own README, not an orphan
fp_reason: README.md explicitly documents manual installation via cp -r, so absence from plugin.json is intentional design, not a broken reference
rule_gap: Cross-component orphan check should exclude components whose own README documents an alternate, non-plugin.json installation path
